title: OnOptions
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: In less than a year, Trump erased 12 years of solvency for the trust fund that pays for Medicare Part A
items.description: The CBO estimates that benefit reductions would start at 8% in 2040 and steadily climb to a 10% cut by 2056.

items.title: This Single ChatGPT Prompt Can Transform Your Important Emails—and Get 10x Better Results
items.description: Catherine Goetze, known as CatGPT, says a simply structured prompt can take ChatGPT from light editor to strategic thinking partner.

items.title: Micron’s $100 billion New York semiconductor manufacturing project is facing local pushback
items.description: On a snowy Friday in January, dignitaries from both political parties braved the chill of a central New York winter for the groundbreaking ceremony of...

items.title: Experts Warn of Unknown Dangers in Viral Peptide Craze
items.description: Without clinical trials, it’s not clear whether the peptides are safe.

items.title: Gen Z, desperate to get off their phones, is powering a sewing renaissance
items.description: Other traditional textile art like crocheting, knitting, and embroidery have popped off among Gen Zers.

items.title: Bitcoin stabilizes after tariff whiplash briefly sends price below $65,000
items.description: The latest volatility for the original cryptocurrency follows the Supreme Court’s landmark decision on Friday.

items.title: ‘Maya MacGuineas should be ashamed’: Scott Bessent launches war of words with the nonpartisan think tank that agrees with Trump on tariffs
items.description: For her part, MacGuineas said Bessent’s diatribe was “a bit of an odd response.”

items.description: Dario Amodei , CEO of Anthropic, will head to the Pentagon on Tuesday to meet with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth about how the military uses the comp...

items.title: AI agents that do your work while you sleep sound great. The reality is far messier—‘it’s like a toddler that needs to be overseen’
items.description: Early adopters using tools like OpenClaw and Claude Code say always-on AI is improving—but experts say they are still far from totally hands-off.

items.title: Private Equity Is Finally Recovering—but Investor Returns Just Hit a Record Low
items.description: A new report from Bain & Company has the good news—and the bad.

items.title: This Controversial Fundraising Hack Is Booming—but It Might Not Be Smart for Your Startup
items.description: A new report found that some AI startups are using a multitiered fundraising maneuver to inflate their valuations. Here’s how it works.

items.title: The Number of Robots and AI Agents Will Soon ‘Explode,’ Outpacing Human Workers
items.description: The prediction about robots and AI agents comes from the head of innovation at Citi Global Insights.
